title: eremophila viscida
intro: Eremophila viscida, also known as Sticky Emu Bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Eremophila viscida include its ability to soothe and calm skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical application, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
